reg1.claimsImplication: Claims received through digital insurer platforms must comply with the same settlement timelines as traditional insurers. Digital documentation (photos, e-signatures) is now explicitly recognised as valid. Review whether your claims intake systems accept digitally-submitted evidence.
reg1.complianceImplication: Any digital distribution channel — app, web portal, chatbot — now requires QCB Digital Insurer licence or operates as a digital window under your existing licence. Board approval required for digital window designation. QCB approval of digital policy wordings is mandatory before launch.
reg1.underwritingImplication: All digital product wordings require QCB approval before launch; review pipeline products immediately. Digital window designation needs board approval; initiate governance process now.
reg1.reinsuranceImplication: Reinsurance treaties must cover risks bound via digital channels; confirm treaty wording explicitly includes digital origination. Notify reinsurers of digital window designation to avoid coverage disputes.
reg1.investmentsImplication: Digital insurer licensing may require separate capital allocation; assess whether digital window affects solvency thresholds. Confirm QCB investment regulations apply equally to digitally-sourced premium pools.

reg2.claimsImplication: Claims processing likely qualifies as an important business service under CTRL. Firms must demonstrate claims operations can remain within impact tolerances during severe disruption. Document claims system resilience testing outcomes.
reg2.complianceImplication: QFC-authorised insurers must have completed important business service identification and impact tolerance setting by 1 October 2025. If not completed, the firm is already in breach. Conduct gap assessment immediately and submit remediation plan to QFCRA. Controlled Functions holders are personally accountable.
reg2.underwritingImplication: Identify whether underwriting platforms qualify as important business services under CTRL. Set and document impact tolerances for underwriting system outages immediately.
reg2.reinsuranceImplication: Reinsurance placement workflows must be assessed as potential important business services under CTRL. Test and document resilience of cedant reporting and treaty systems against impact tolerances.
reg2.investmentsImplication: Investment execution and portfolio management systems must be reviewed against CTRL important business service criteria. Document impact tolerances for trading platform disruptions and complete resilience testing now.

reg3.claimsImplication: Climate-related claims (flood, storm, property) must now be reported as climate-related financial disclosures under IFRS S2. Quantify and disclose the financial impact of climate-related claims in annual sustainability reports.
reg3.complianceImplication: QFC-authorised insurers must publish first sustainability reports under IFRS S1/S2 for the 2025 financial year. Confirm whether your existing sustainability reporting qualifies as "highly aligned" with IFRS S1/S2 or whether a separate QFC-format report is required. Annual MLRO Report deadline: 28 February 2026.
reg3.underwritingImplication: Underwriters must now capture and tag climate-exposed risks (flood, storm, property) to enable IFRS S2 financial impact disclosures. Update policy data fields immediately to support 2025 annual sustainability report quantification requirements.
reg3.reinsuranceImplication: Reinsurance structures must be reviewed to evidence climate risk transfer under IFRS S2 disclosure obligations. Treaty terms and recoveries on climate-related losses must be separately quantifiable for branch-level reporting.
reg3.investmentsImplication: Investment portfolios must be assessed for climate-related financial risks and opportunities under IFRS S1 general disclosure requirements. Report climate exposure metrics by asset class to meet the 2025 QFC sustainability report deadline.

reg4.claimsImplication: Claims handlers must be included in AML/CFT training records. Large claims payments above threshold must be screened for suspicious transaction indicators. Ensure claims team is included in the MLRO's assessment of high-risk customer interactions.
reg4.complianceImplication: Annual MLRO Report for 2024/25 must be submitted by 28 February 2026. The MLRO must assess all AML/CFT controls including those applied in claims and underwriting. The report goes to the Board, not directly to QFCRA. Non-submission is a breach of QFCRA rules.
reg4.underwritingImplication: Underwriters must ensure customer risk assessments are documented for MLRO review by February 2026. Flag high-risk policyholders explicitly in underwriting files to support MLRO's AML/CFT control assessment.
reg4.reinsuranceImplication: Reinsurance counterparty due diligence records must be available for MLRO review under AML/CFT controls. Ensure treaty and facultative files document sanctions screening conducted at placement.
reg4.investmentsImplication: Investment counterparty and asset manager screening records must be included in MLRO's AML/CFT controls assessment. Ensure all transaction monitoring logs are audit-ready before the February 2026 submission deadline.

reg5.claimsImplication: Arabic is the default policy language — discrepancies resolved in favour of Arabic text for all UAE-governed claims. Ensure claims handlers are aware English translations are not authoritative if Arabic text differs. Claims payments must go directly from insurer to policyholder.
reg5.complianceImplication: All UAE non-financial free zone operations (DMCC, JAFZA etc.) must have CBUAE NOC in place — DMCC deadline was 31 December 2025. Check status immediately. Penalties for unlicensed activity: up to AED 500 million. Transition deadline: 16 September 2026.
reg5.underwritingImplication: Review all UAE policy wordings immediately to ensure Arabic text is authoritative and controls in disputes. Reassess UAE risk appetite given technology-neutral licensing and new ESG underwriting criteria before September 2026.
reg5.reinsuranceImplication: Audit reinsurance treaties covering UAE risks for Arabic-language policy conflicts that may affect claims recovery. Confirm reinsurers accept direct insurer-to-policyholder payment structures under the consolidated framework.
reg5.investmentsImplication: Align UAE investment mandates with explicit ESG objectives introduced under the consolidated statute before September 2026. Verify any free zone investment vehicles hold valid CBUAE NOC to avoid AED 500 million penalties.

reg6.claimsImplication: Direct implication: claim payments must now be paid directly by the insurer to the policyholder. If your claims process routes payments through a broker, this is non-compliant in UAE. Review all UAE claims payment workflows immediately.
reg6.complianceImplication: All UAE broker relationships must be reviewed for compliance with the new Regulation. Brokers under pre-2025 frameworks are in breach. CBUAE has signalled tougher enforcement — sanctions include withdrawing broker authorisation.
reg6.underwritingImplication: Review all UAE broker agreements to ensure claim payments route directly to policyholders, not brokers. Update policy wordings and payment workflows for UAE risks immediately.
reg6.reinsuranceImplication: Confirm reinsurance recoveries on UAE risks are not channelled through brokers acting as intermediaries. Adjust treaty and facultative settlement instructions accordingly.
reg6.investmentsImplication: No material implication for this department.

reg7.claimsImplication: Any UAE policyholder dispute must go to Sanadak before litigation. Internal complaints process must be completed and documented before Sanadak referral. Ensure UAE claims handlers know the Sanadak referral process.
reg7.complianceImplication: Confirm UAE internal complaints procedures are compliant with Sanadak referral requirements. Any attempt to commence UAE court proceedings without prior Sanadak referral will be procedurally defective.
reg7.underwritingImplication: Review UAE policy wordings to ensure dispute resolution clauses reference Sanadak, not the defunct IDC. Update UAE proposal forms and policy documents before binding new risks.
reg7.reinsuranceImplication: No material implication for this department.
reg7.investmentsImplication: No material implication for this department.

reg8.claimsImplication: Claims recoveries on Saudi risks may now involve local Saudi reinsurers as lead or follow reinsurers. Ensure claims reinsurance recovery processes account for local Saudi reinsurer participation.
reg8.complianceImplication: If your company writes KSA risks or operates a KSA branch, 30% of all reinsurance treaties must have been offered to local Saudi reinsurance market from 1 January 2025. Document the offer even if local market declines. SAMA audit risk is elevated.
reg8.underwritingImplication: Review all KSA-licensed business to confirm treaty structures comply with 30% local retention from January 2025. Flag any lines where local reinsurer capacity may constrain treaty placement or pricing.
reg8.reinsuranceImplication: Audit all proportional and non-proportional KSA treaties immediately to evidence formal offers to local Saudi reinsurers. Maintain documented proof of offer and response for SAMA audit readiness.
reg8.investmentsImplication: Increased local Saudi reinsurer participation may affect counterparty credit exposure and cash flow timing on recoveries. Review credit risk concentrations against approved reinsurer panels for Saudi-domiciled entities.

reg9.claimsImplication: All Qatar-jurisdiction claims must be handled under QCB-regulated framework. Policy wordings for Qatar risks must have been QCB-approved. If a claim arises on a non-approved wording, coverage may be disputed.
reg9.complianceImplication: Quarterly prudential returns and annual compliance declarations must be submitted to QCB Insurance Supervision Department. All active policies covering Qatar risks must be on QCB-approved wordings.
reg9.underwritingImplication: All Qatar-risk policies must use QCB-approved wordings; audit existing portfolio for non-compliant placements immediately. Decline or remediate any submission where Article 95 non-admitted exposure is identified.
reg9.reinsuranceImplication: Reinsurance treaties must align with QCB framework; non-admitted outward placements covering Qatar-located assets require urgent review. Confirm all facultative and treaty structures comply with Article 95 before next renewal.
reg9.investmentsImplication: Annual Board solvency assessment requires investment assets to demonstrably support QCB-mandated capital adequacy thresholds. Review asset allocation quarterly to ensure liquidity positions satisfy prudential return submissions.

reg10.claimsImplication: Under IFRS 17, the liability for incurred claims (LIC) is separately measured and disclosed. Claims teams must provide more granular data on claims development to actuarial/finance teams.
reg10.complianceImplication: QFC-authorised insurers must be filing IFRS 17 compliant accounts. If still using IFRS 4 transitional provisions, you are likely in breach. Confirm with finance whether IFRS 17 implementation is complete for 2024 annual accounts.
reg10.underwritingImplication: Underwriting must supply granular premium allocation and coverage boundary data to support CSM calculations. Review contract groupings now to ensure IFRS 17 cohort definitions align with your portfolio segmentation.
reg10.reinsuranceImplication: Reinsurance contracts held must be measured separately under IFRS 17, potentially altering net liability presentation. Confirm reinsurance treaty data feeds actuarial models correctly for 2024 reporting.
reg10.investmentsImplication: Investment income disclosure requirements tighten under IFRS 17 due to the disaggregation of insurance and finance results. Ensure investment return data is structured to support the Insurance Finance Income or Expense split.

live_reg7.claimsImplication: New claims handling requirements or reporting obligations may be published; review updates to ensure claims processes remain compliant with QCB expectations.
live_reg7.complianceImplication: Conduct immediate review of QCB publications page to identify any new directives requiring implementation and update compliance calendar accordingly.
live_reg7.underwritingImplication: Review QCB publications immediately for new underwriting or product guidelines effective 26 March 2026. Adjust policy wordings or risk acceptance criteria if new circulars mandate changes.
live_reg7.reinsuranceImplication: Check QCB notices for updated reinsurance placement or retention requirements affecting treaty structures. Notify reinsurance partners promptly if new obligations alter cession terms.
live_reg7.investmentsImplication: Scan QCB guidance for revised capital or asset allocation requirements impacting the investment portfolio. Realign investment strategy before the 26 March effective date if new limits are published.
